title: Core Concepts Path
description: Build fluency in Core Concepts inside Foundations by moving from foundational terms into adjacent methods, variants, and extensions.

steps.title: Optimization Algorithms
steps.summary: Algorithms used to minimize or maximize a objective function, such as stochastic gradient descent, Adam, and RMSProp. It sits inside Foundations / Core Concepts. Compare it with Model and Machine Learning. It opens into MAP Optimization and Meta-Optimization.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Machine Learning
steps.summary: Machine Learning belongs to the Foundations area of AI/ML, within Core Concepts. It sits inside Foundations / Core Concepts. Start after Q-Learning and V-Learning. It opens into Machine Learning (ML) and Azure Machine Learning.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Machine Learning (ML)
steps.summary: Machine Learning (ML) belongs to the Foundations area of AI/ML, within Core Concepts. It sits inside Foundations / Core Concepts. Start after Machine Learning and ML Systems.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
steps.summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) belongs to the Foundations area of AI/ML, within Core Concepts. It sits inside Foundations / Core Concepts. Start after AI in Architecture and AI and Architecture.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Model
steps.summary: Model belongs to the Foundations area of AI/ML, within Core Concepts. It sits inside Foundations / Core Concepts. Compare it with Algorithm and Optimization Algorithms.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Algorithm
steps.summary: Algorithm belongs to the Foundations area of AI/ML, within Core Concepts. It sits inside Foundations / Core Concepts. Compare it with Model and Optimization Algorithms.
steps.whyIncluded: Treat this as an advanced extension once the earlier terms feel natural.
